title: Sprites Gallery page for Pokemon static sprite comparison

stories.title: Create sprite-sources.ts data module
acceptanceCriteria: src/data/sprite-sources.ts exists with SpriteSource interface and SPRITE_SOURCES array
All 9 sources are configured: default, shiny, gen1-rb, gen1-yellow, gen2-gold, gen2-silver, home, dream-world, official-artwork
maxId boundaries are set correctly for gen1 (151), gen2 (251), dream-world (649)
isValidForSource(id, source) helper correctly validates bounds
No GIF sources are included; only PNG and SVG

stories.title: Create sprites-gallery.astro page
acceptanceCriteria: src/pages/sprites-gallery.astro exists and uses BaseLayout.astro
Control bar has ID input (1-1025), random button, generation filter dropdown
Responsive grid displays sprites (mobile 2 cols -> desktop 6 cols)
Each card shows sprite image (original size, pixelated), source name, format/size/style tags, copy-URL button
Invalid IDs or out-of-generation-range IDs show a placeholder (gray box + question mark)
Keyboard shortcuts work: arrow keys change ID, R randomizes, F sets favicon
Bottom code snippet shows the URL template for the currently selected source
npm run build passes with 0 errors

stories.title: Wire favicon preference from Gallery into BaseLayout
acceptanceCriteria: BaseLayout.astro reads preferred-sprite-source and preferred-pokemon-id from localStorage
If a preference exists, it overrides the random favicon logic
Gallery 'Set as favicon' button stores the preference and updates the current page favicon immediately
npm run build passes with 0 errors

stories.title: Styling matches site theme and dark mode
acceptanceCriteria: Gallery uses site color tokens and supports dark/light mode
Retro sprites (gen1/gen2) use CSS scale(1.5) with transform-origin center
All images use image-rendering: pixelated / crisp-edges
Card hover shows subtle shadow and reveals technical details
No visual regressions in existing pages
